metadata.description: Golden cases for pediatric toxicology assessment
metadata.source: Rumack-Matthew nomogram, Done's salicylate classification, Tenenbein iron toxicity, Drug-Induced Liver Injury Network

toxicology_golden_cases.clinical_scenario: 5-year-old with accidental acetaminophen overdose, 4 hours post-ingestion, plasma level 12 mcg/mL
toxicology_golden_cases.source: Rumack-Matthew nomogram, below treatment line at 4 hours
toxicology_golden_cases.calculation_method: Plasma level 12 mcg/mL is below threshold of 30 mcg/mL at 4 hours
toxicology_golden_cases.notes: Below treatment line; supportive care and monitoring sufficient

toxicology_golden_cases.clinical_scenario: 8-year-old with acetaminophen toxicity, 4 hours post-ingestion, plasma level 28 mcg/mL
toxicology_golden_cases.calculation_method: Plasma level 28 mcg/mL near threshold of 30 mcg/mL at 4 hours
toxicology_golden_cases.notes: In possible toxicity zone; consider NAC for hepatoprotection

toxicology_golden_cases.clinical_scenario: 6-year-old with significant acetaminophen toxicity, 2 hours post-ingestion, plasma level 120 mcg/mL
toxicology_golden_cases.calculation_method: Plasma level 120 mcg/mL above threshold (~100 mcg/mL at 2 hours)
toxicology_golden_cases.notes: Intermediate risk for hepatotoxicity; NAC indicated

toxicology_golden_cases.clinical_scenario: 10-year-old with mild aspirin toxicity, plasma salicylate 22 mg/dL
toxicology_golden_cases.calculation_method: Plasma level 22 mg/dL in mild range (15-30 mg/dL)
toxicology_golden_cases.notes: Mild toxicity with early respiratory alkalosis; supportive care

toxicology_golden_cases.clinical_scenario: 9-year-old with severe salicylate toxicity, plasma level 75 mg/dL, acidotic
toxicology_golden_cases.source: Done's salicylate classification, late metabolic acidosis
toxicology_golden_cases.calculation_method: Plasma level 75 mg/dL in severe range (60-100 mg/dL); metabolic acidosis present
toxicology_golden_cases.notes: Severe toxicity with metabolic acidosis; hemodialysis indicated; intensive monitoring

toxicology_golden_cases.clinical_scenario: 7-year-old with iron ingestion, serum iron 120 mcg/dL, TIBC 350 mcg/dL
toxicology_golden_cases.source: Iron toxicity assessment, low iron level
toxicology_golden_cases.calculation_method: Serum iron 120 mcg/dL in mild range (100-300); iron-TIBC ratio 0.34
toxicology_golden_cases.notes: Mild iron toxicity; supportive care; monitor for progression

toxicology_golden_cases.clinical_scenario: 6-year-old with significant iron overdose, serum iron 680 mcg/dL, TIBC 350 mcg/dL, 4 hours post-ingestion
toxicology_golden_cases.source: Iron toxicity with iron exceeding TIBC
toxicology_golden_cases.calculation_method: Serum iron 680 mcg/dL well above 500 threshold; iron-TIBC ratio 1.94 >1.0
toxicology_golden_cases.notes: Severe iron toxicity; immediate deferoxamine chelation indicated

toxicology_golden_cases.clinical_scenario: 11-year-old on isoniazid for TB prophylaxis, routine monitoring, normal LFTs
toxicology_golden_cases.source: Normal hepatic function on drug therapy
toxicology_golden_cases.calculation_method: ALT 28 within normal range; normal synthetic function markers
toxicology_golden_cases.notes: No evidence of drug-induced liver injury; continue therapy with routine monitoring

toxicology_golden_cases.clinical_scenario: 14-year-old on anti-TB therapy, elevated transaminases, ALT 180 IU/L, normal bilirubin

expected_output.recommendation: Monitor LFTs, consider continuing drug with close follow-up

toxicology_golden_cases.source: Drug-induced transaminitis with preserved synthetic function
toxicology_golden_cases.calculation_method: ALT 180 is 4.5x upper normal (40); grade 2 hepatotoxicity; preserved synthetic function
toxicology_golden_cases.notes: Moderate elevation but low-risk; monitor closely; most likely resolves with continued observation

toxicology_golden_cases.clinical_scenario: 10-year-old with acute liver injury on amoxicillin-clavulanate, ALT 520, total bilirubin 3.2, INR 2.1

expected_output.recommendation: Hold drug immediately, intensive care management

toxicology_golden_cases.source: Severe drug-induced liver injury with coagulopathy
toxicology_golden_cases.calculation_method: ALT 520 >20x normal (40); bilirubin >3 raises to grade 4; impaired synthetic function (INR 2.1)
toxicology_golden_cases.notes: High-risk hepatotoxicity; discontinue drug immediately; hepatology + ICU consultation

toxicology_golden_cases.clinical_scenario: 4-year-old with accidental acetaminophen ingestion, 500 mg reported
toxicology_golden_cases.source: Toxic ingestion assessment, sub-toxic acetaminophen dose
toxicology_golden_cases.calculation_method: Dose 500 mg = 31 mg/kg; below toxic threshold of 150 mg/kg
toxicology_golden_cases.notes: Non-toxic ingestion; no specific therapy needed; consider activated charcoal if within 1-2 hours

toxicology_golden_cases.clinical_scenario: 6-year-old with iron supplement bottle ingestion, estimated 1200 mg dose
toxicology_golden_cases.calculation_method: Dose 1200 mg = 60 mg/kg; far exceeds toxic threshold of 20 mg/kg
toxicology_golden_cases.notes: High-risk iron poisoning; activated charcoal not effective; may require chelation depending on levels

tolerance_explanation.rationale: Clinical assessment with appropriate tolerance for measurement variation (0.5%). Different interpretation for categorical vs numeric:
factors: Categorical outputs (toxicity_category, risk_zone): tolerance=0.0 (must match exactly)
Ratios and derived values: tolerance as specified per test
